Add unset capability for ParmParse (#5263)

## Summary

For ParmParse, once a variable is set in an input file, there is no way
to remove it completely from the command line or subsequently in the
input file. For example, you may have specified `amr.keyword = 5` in
`inputs.inp` but then want to override it on the command line so a check
on `pp.contains("amr.keyword")` comes up as false. Right now, using
command line input overrides you can set it to a null value but not
completely remove it from the table (pp.remove() can remove from the
table in source code).

This PR adds an UNSET directive to ParmParse that removes a
previously-defined keyword from the parse table. This enables input
files to conditionally suppress parameters — for example, an included
file can cancel a key set by its parent.
```
amr.keyword = 5
UNSET = amr.keyword
# equivalent to never having set amr.keyword
```

Multiple keys can be removed in a single directive:
```
UNSET = key1 key2 key3
```

## Additional background

Motivated by discussion in
https://github.com/Exawind/amr-wind/pull/1897. Code generated by
Copilot.

Mirrors the design of the existing FILE directive. The new
`ParmParse::UnsetKeyword = "UNSET"` static is exposed in the header for
symmetry with FileKeyword. The directive is handled in addDefn() by
erasing each listed key from the table; keys not present are silently
ignored.

Tests added to Tests/ParmParse/inputs and Tests/ParmParse/main.cpp cover
single-key unset, multi-key unset, and verifying that non-listed keys
are unaffected.

## Checklist

The proposed changes:
- [ ] fix a bug or incorrect behavior in AMReX
- [X] add new capabilities to AMReX
- [ ] changes answers in the test suite to more than roundoff level
- [ ] are likely to significantly affect the results of downstream AMReX
users
- [X] include documentation in the code and/or rst files, if appropriate

---------

✨ feat: React Compiler + MUI theme bug fixes (#35)

* ✨ feat: enable React Compiler in development mode

Add bun-plugin-react-compiler to bunfig.toml [serve.static] plugins so
the React Compiler runs during hot-reload dev mode, matching the
babel-plugin-react-compiler transform already applied in build.ts for
production builds.

- Install bun-plugin-react-compiler@0.3.2 as devDependency
- Add plugin to [serve.static] in bunfig.toml
- Whitelist in knip.json (referenced by name in .toml, not imported in TS)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* 📝 docs: document React Compiler environments in frontend README

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ♻️ refactor: revert bun-plugin-react-compiler — incompatible with MUI sx

bun-plugin-react-compiler runs babel-plugin-react-compiler without
@babel/preset-react, which causes the React Compiler to transform MUI
sx theme callbacks in a way that loses custom palette tokens (gradient,
surface, etc.) at runtime.

React Compiler coverage remains:
- Production: babel-plugin-react-compiler via custom Bun plugin in build.ts
- Lint: eslint-plugin-react-compiler enforces compliance in bun run cc

Dev hot-reload intentionally does not run the compiler.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* 🐛 fix: replace sx theme callbacks with useTheme() for custom palette tokens

MUI v7 with cssVariables: true passes a CSS variables proxy as the
theme argument to sx function callbacks. Custom palette extensions
(gradient, surface, sidebar) are not registered in MUI's CSS variables
system, so theme.palette.gradient is undefined in that context.

Fix: call useTheme() in the component body and reference palette values
directly as strings in sx — works correctly with CSS variables, React
Compiler, and all render contexts.

Affected components:
- surfaceCard.tsx: background callback for tone === 'accent'
- leftNav.tsx (LeftNavContent): gradient.hero background + gradient.accent backgroundImage
- notFoundPage.tsx: gradient.accent for text clip background

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* 🐛 fix: remove cssVariables: true — breaks custom palette tokens at runtime

In MUI v7, cssVariables: true transforms theme.palette so that standard
MUI tokens become CSS variable references. Custom palette extensions
(gradient, surface, border, sidebar) are not part of MUI's CSS variables
registry, so they are inaccessible at theme.palette.* in components —
useTheme() returns undefined for them.

Since theme.vars is never consumed in any component (only asserted in a
single test), cssVariables: true provides no benefit and causes the
runtime crash.

- Remove cssVariables: true from createTheme() in theme.ts
- Drop the theme.vars assertion from the theme test

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* 🐛 fix: resolve theme tokens before passing to alpha() in StatCard

alpha() is a pure CSS color utility — it cannot parse MUI dot-notation
token strings like 'primary.main'. The toneStyles map was using these
strings directly as arguments to alpha(), producing invalid/crashing
output.

Fix: use useTheme() to resolve each token to an actual colour value
before passing it to alpha(). Also simplifies the component by
collapsing the bg/fg pair into a single bg colour (fg was always
'#ffffff').

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

backends via a side config file (emulator-config.json), CLI --storage
option, or the Explorer settings page.

Configuration:
- Add StorageType enum (SurrealDb, Sqlite, InMemory)
- Add Storage property to EmulatorOptions
- Load emulator-config.json from executable directory at startup
- Add --storage CLI option to the start command
- Priority: appsettings.json < emulator-config.json < CLI args

Storage backends:
- InMemoryDocumentStore: ConcurrentDictionary-based, ephemeral
- SqliteDocumentStore: Microsoft.Data.Sqlite, single-file persistence
- SqliteChangeFeedProvider: persistent change feed in SQLite
- InMemory/SQLite activity and query telemetry stores
- DocumentStoreHelpers: shared logic for partition keys, patches, etc.
- StorageServiceRegistration: DI dispatch based on StorageType

Explorer settings UI:
- Storage Configuration card with backend dropdown and data directory
- GET/PUT /api/emulator/config endpoint for emulator-config.json
- Restart-required messaging after saving storage changes
- Storage type shown in the Configuration info card

- Enforce framer-motion animations for all React/Next.js projects

**Dependencies Added:**

This should result in significantly more polished and animated generated UIs.
